title: Delicate Flaky Norwegian Cod Casserole
description: A briny and flaky Norwegian dish featuring cod, stoned dates, and caramelized flavors, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This pescatarian-friendly casserole is a delightful twist on traditional Norwegian cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until caramelized.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
In a separate saucepan, whisk together the flour and milk, and cook over medium heat until the mixture thickens.
Season the cod fillets with salt and black pepper.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange a layer of cod fillets.
Spoon the caramelized onion and garlic mixture over the cod, followed by a layer of stoned dates.
Pour the flour and milk mixture over the dates, and sprinkle with grated cheddar cheese if using.
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the cod is flaky and the cheese is golden brown.
